openingMessage: The most important thing a leader can do is create an environment where everyone feels they are part of the team and the team is part of them.
openingWhy: The hand who feels like an outsider won't call stop-work — he'll hope somebody senior does it first. The crew where the greenest worm will grab an operator's sleeve and say 'walk me through this' is the crew that catches what everybody else walks right past.
leadershipThought: Distraction, fatigue, and rushing are the three passengers that never announce themselves. Any one of them alone is a stop condition. Check for all three before the truck moves — not after the first close call.
exxonMoments:
Set up for vehicle placement went well with spotters backing them in [Ralph Forbes] (Driving)
Set up for vehicle placement went well with spotters backing them in

Exclusion Zone Breach — Third-Party Personnel (Near Miss) (Line of Fire)
When SimOps create unplanned traffic near your exclusion zone, proactive interception by all crew members — not just the safety tech — is what prevents a line-of-fire exposure.

Communication with crew and MT was good talked about potential hazards [Raymundo Solis] (Operational Discipline)

fieldNews.headline: SM Energy #112 — 6,000 ppm H2S / 2% CO2 (carry-forward from 7/10)
fieldNews.detail: Michael Hutt reported it Friday. Confirm current readings before any entry or first break this week.

fieldNews.headline: Two driving near-misses flagged Friday — stay heads-up on the road
fieldNews.detail: Jordan Lemmons (swerving driver, Jal 128) and Alex Clubb (service truck into his lane at 75 mph). The drive is part of the job.

todaysChallenge: Your standard today protects people you'll never meet — the night crew, the pumper tomorrow, somebody's dad next month. Leave the location better because you were on it.
todaysChallengeSubtext: “A life is not important except in the impact it has on other lives.” — Jackie Robinson
